- Goal model: Consume all colored bars by clicking on matching U-shapes in containers. Each click removes a chunk (3 units) from the bar.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I'm at 36 actions and still not completed. Every click only changes 1 cell (the HUD bar). I need to completely rethink this problem.
Let me consider: what if the goal is to click on the G (dark gray) frame pieces of the bars? These are the 3-pixel G objects at the ends of the bars.
- id=1: G, 3 pixels, boundary [[9, 27], [11, 27]] - left end of green bar
- id=15: G, 3 pixels, boundary [[27, 9], [27, 11]] - top end of yellow bar
Wait, actually these might be the "handles" or "ends" of the bars. What if clicking on them moves the bars?
Let me try clicking on the G frame piece at the end of the green bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the G frame pieces at the ends of the bars
# id=1: G, 3 pixels, boundary [[9, 27], [11, 27]] - left end of green bar
# id=15: G, 3 pixels, boundary [[27, 9], [27, 11]] - top end of yellow bar
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 27},  # click left end of green bar
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
